section.heading: Wildlife corridors

paragraph.content: assess the impacts of habitat fragmentation on wildlife in the California Desert Conservation Area; and
establish policies and procedures to ensure the preservation of wildlife corridors and facilitate species migration.

content.p: As soon as practicable, but not later than 2 years, after March 12, 2019, the Secretary shall complete a study regarding the impact of habitat fragmentation on wildlife in the California Desert Conservation Area.

subparagraph.content: identify the species migrating, or likely to migrate 11 So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma. in the California Desert Conservation Area;

subparagraph.chapeau: examine the impacts and potential impacts of habitat fragmentation on—

subparagraph.content: identify critical wildlife and species migration corridors recommended for preservation; and
include recommendations for ensuring the biological connectivity of public land managed by the Secretary and the Secretary of Defense throughout the California Desert Conservation Area.

paragraph.heading: Rights-of-way
paragraph.chapeau: The Secretary shall consider the information and recommendations of the study under paragraph (1) to determine the individual and cumulative impacts of rights-of-way for projects in the California Desert Conservation Area, in accordance with—

subparagraph.content: the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. 4321 et seq.);
the Endangered Species Act of 1973 (16 U.S.C. 1531 et seq.); and

content.p: The Secretary shall incorporate into all land management plans applicable to the California Desert Conservation Area the findings and recommendations of the study completed under subsection (b).

section.sourceCredit: (Pub. L. 103–433, title VII, § 713, as added Pub. L. 116–9, title I, § 1452, Mar. 12, 2019, 133 Stat. 711.)

note.heading: References in Text
note.p: The National Environmental Policy Act of 1969, referred to in subsec. (b)(3)(A), is Pub. L. 91–190, Jan. 1, 1970, 83 Stat. 852, which is classified generally to chapter 55 (§ 4321 et seq.) of Title 42, The Public Health and Welfare. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 4321 of Title 42 and Tables.

The Endangered Species Act of 1973, referred to in subsec. (b)(3)(B), is Pub. L. 93–205, Dec. 28, 1973, 87 Stat. 884, which is classified generally to chapter 35 (§ 1531 et seq.) of this title. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under section 1531 of this title and Tables.

note.heading: “Secretary” Defined
note.p: Section 103 of Pub. L. 103–433 provided in part that in this subchapter “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.
